steps.title: Represent the starting counts
steps.content: Let each species have $x$ insects at the start. A $2{,}000\%$ increase means adding $20x$, so the ending prey count is $21x$. A $250\%$ increase means adding $2.5x$, so the ending predator count is $3.5x$.

steps.title: Compare ending counts
steps.content:
The ratio of ending prey to ending predators is
$$\frac{21x}{3.5x}=6$$
So the ending prey count is $6$ times the ending predator count.

steps.title: Convert the ratio to percent greater
steps.content:
Being $6$ times as large means being $5$ times greater than the comparison amount.
$$5\cdot 100\%=500\%$$
The answer is 500.
